name: Ermita de San Valero and Río Cinca Loop
name_original: Ermita de San Valero por el Valle del Río Cinca — circular por Velilla de Cinca
description: Explore the easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) Ermita de San Valero circular hike in Velilla de Cinca, featuring historic ruins and river valley views.
region_intro: Hiking around Velilla De Cinca offers routes through a landscape characterized by the Cinca River, agricultural areas, and gentle elevations. The region features paths that follow riverbanks and traverse open countryside, providing views of the local environment. Trails often include sections near cultivated fields and small settlements. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, with modest elevation changes. Best hiking trails around Velilla De Cinca The most popular hiking route is Ponent Trail: Saidí Loop, a 6.2 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain through the local area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de San Valero and Río Cinca Loop, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail follows the Cinca River, providing riverside views and access to local landmarks. Local hikers also love the Finca San Simón loop from Barrio San Simón, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through agricultural land and offering some elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Velilla De Cinca is defined by riverine paths, agricultural landscapes, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate loops with more varied terrain. The routes in Velilla De Cinca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Velilla De Cinca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Velilla De Cinca offers a selection of 10 hiking routes, catering to various preferences. These include 6 easy trails and 4 moderate options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Yes, Velilla De Cinca is well-suited for beginners and families, with 6 easy trails available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086743228/ermita-de-san-valero-and-rio-cinca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Valero and Río Cinca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 miles (6.4 km) long and follows the Cinca River, offering gentle terrain and scenic riverside views.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Hikes in Velilla De Cinca vary in length and duration. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1091901391/ciudad-de-fraga-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Fraga — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 1.9 miles (3.1 km) and takes around 50 minutes. Longer moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finca San Simón loop from Barrio San Simón&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 6.6 miles (10.7 km) and take approximately 3 hours 5 minutes.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Velilla De Cinca are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086770931/ponent-trail-saidi-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponent Trail: Saidí Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086743228/ermita-de-san-valero-and-rio-cinca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Valero and Río Cinca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Velilla De Cinca?
A: The landscape around Velilla De Cinca is characterized by the Cinca River, extensive agricultural areas, and gentle elevations. You'll find paths that follow riverbanks, traverse open countryside, and offer views of cultivated fields and small settlements. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in rural areas like Velilla De Cinca. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural land or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them. Always respect local signage regarding pets.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore several interesting points. The Cinca River itself is a prominent feature, and you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín Church in Chalamera&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraga&lt;/a&gt; also offers a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;viewpoint overlooking the city&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Velilla De Cinca?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking in Velilla De Cinca. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery, including the agricultural landscapes, is particularly pleasant. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cool, so plan accordingly.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Velilla De Cinca in autumn?
A: In autumn, layers are key. Wear comfortable, moisture-wicking clothing, and bring a light jacket or fleece for cooler mornings and evenings. Sturdy, comfortable hiking shoes are essential for navigating varied terrain, and consider a hat and sunglasses for sun protection.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking routes in Velilla De Cinca?
A: Yes, some routes offer scenic viewpoints. For instance, you can find a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;view of the city of Fraga&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a good perspective of the surrounding area and the Cinca River valley.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Velilla De Cinca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil riverine paths, the unique agricultural landscapes, and the accessibility of routes for different ability levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access the trailheads in Velilla De Cinca?
A: While Velilla De Cinca is a smaller community, public transport options might be limited directly to all trailheads. It is generally advisable to check local bus schedules if relying on public transport, or consider driving, as many routes are accessible from or near the village itself.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Velilla De Cinca and nearby settlements like Fraga offer local amenities, including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your hike. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, or pack your own snacks and drinks.
tips: The hermitage that has the name Ermita de San Valero is a small construction of Romanesque origin that was built on the remains of a Roman mausoleum that had been there before, also called San Valero.
Beautiful town located at the foot of Monte de San Valero, a table that has been created by the passage of the Río Cinca through these sedimentary lands.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route is approximately 6.4 kilometers long and typically takes about 1 hour and 39 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. It's a pleasant stroll rather than a strenuous hike.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This trail is classified as an "easy walk" (caminata fácil) with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families looking for a gentle outdoor activity.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ermita de San Valero trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat or gently undulating, consisting of accessible paths. You'll be walking through the Cinca River valley, characterized by agricultural landscapes and rural environments, with no challenging technical sections.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Ermita de San Valero, a Romanesque church built upon the remains of a 1st-century Roman mausoleum. You'll also enjoy views of the Cinca River and extensive agricultural landscapes, including fruit orchards, as you pass through Velilla de Cinca.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, as the route starts and ends in Velilla de Cinca, you should be able to find parking within the village. Look for designated parking areas or suitable street parking near the start of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in rural agricultural areas like this are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural fields or near livestock, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Ermita de San Valero trail?
faqs.answer: Given the agricultural landscape with fruit orchards, spring (when trees are blooming) and early autumn (harvest season) would offer particularly scenic views. The easy nature of the trail makes it enjoyable in most seasons, but avoid the peak heat of summer afternoons.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to visit the Ermita de San Valero. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 14% of this route overlaps with sections of the official trail known as "Contrastes del Bajo Cinca".

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is circular and passes through Velilla de Cinca, you may find local amenities such as cafes or small shops within the village before or after your hike. There are no specific facilities directly on the trail itself.

faqs.question: What makes the Ermita de San Valero particularly interesting?
faqs.answer: The Ermita de San Valero is unique because it was built in the 13th century directly on the remains of a 1st-century Roman mausoleum. You can still see large, profiled Roman ashlar stones in its foundation. It also has an unusual inverted liturgical orientation and remnants of Roman tessellated pavement near its entrance.
